easy.question: What is the purpose of the Fire Safety Policy?
easy.answer: To outline Midland Heart’s approach to managing fire risk and implementing fire precautions across premises, protecting staff, tenants, visitors, contractors and communities. :contentReference[oaicite:69]{index=69}

easy.question: Which locations are in scope?
easy.answer: Independent/Retirement/Specialist Accommodation (incl. communal areas), offices/ storage, leasehold/mutual schemes where we retain responsibility, general needs, and agency-managed/leased properties where we retain responsibility. :contentReference[oaicite:70]{index=70}

easy.question: How can concerns be raised and how is social media handled?
easy.answer: Through any channel (staff, phone, email, social media). Social posts receive a private reply to maintain confidentiality. :contentReference[oaicite:71]{index=71}

easy.question: What reasonable-adjustment principle applies to access for fire checks?
easy.answer: We will discuss needs and make reasonable adjustments (e.g., flexible appointments) to facilitate access; decisions consider individual circumstances. :contentReference[oaicite:72]{index=72}

easy.question: Name three Fire Performance Standards requirements.
easy.answer: Carry out suitable FRAs; provide residents with fire safety instructions (and fire-door check requirements where there are common areas); and complete fire-door checks (11m+ annually for front doors and quarterly for communal doors). :contentReference[oaicite:73]{index=73}

easy.question: List two key HRRB (18m+/7+ storeys) duties.
easy.answer: Register with the Building Safety Regulator; obtain and display a Building Assessment Certificate; also provide a Secure Information Box, plans, wayfinding signage, and maintain firefighters lifts. :contentReference[oaicite:74]{index=74}

easy.question: Who are examples of ‘Responsible Persons’ by area?
easy.answer: General Needs: Head of Housing Management; Retirement Living/Specialist: Head of Retirement Living; Independent Living: Head of Lettings & Tenancy Sustainment; Head Offices: Head of Safety & Facilities; etc. :contentReference[oaicite:75]{index=75}

easy.question: What is Midland Heart’s stance when things go wrong?
easy.answer: Accept responsibility and put things right quickly. :contentReference[oaicite:76]{index=76}

medium.question: A resident posts a corridor fire-door concern on Facebook. What is the correct handling and follow-up?
medium.answer: Respond privately to protect confidentiality, log the concern, arrange inspection, and ensure communal fire-door checks are up to date per frequency; record any mitigation actions. :contentReference[oaicite:77]{index=77}

medium.question: What routine activities evidence ongoing compliance beyond the FRA itself?
medium.answer: Regular testing/recording of fire precautions; mandatory staff training; housekeeping checks; and recording/ updating mitigation action plans. :contentReference[oaicite:78]{index=78}

medium.question: A building is 10 storeys. Summarise extra obligations that apply.
medium.answer: As an HRRB (7+ storeys/18m+), register with BSR, obtain/display BAC, provide SIB, consider external wall design/materials in FRAs, provide floor/building plans, maintain firefighting equipment and wayfinding signage. :contentReference[oaicite:79]{index=79}

medium.question: What is meant by ‘best endeavours’ for resident front-door checks?
medium.answer: Three attempts to inspect a fire door, each with written communication asking the resident to arrange an appointment. :contentReference[oaicite:80]{index=80}

medium.question: Which documents/templates support operational delivery (name two)?
medium.answer: Fire Risk Action Extension Request; Stay Put/Full Evacuation Notices; PEEP templates; FRA Process Maps; Weekly Fire Door Report; Building ERP template. :contentReference[oaicite:81]{index=81}

medium.question: How are advocates/MPs handled when raising concerns on a resident’s behalf?
medium.answer: Advocates/support agencies require the resident’s written consent; MPs/Councillors do not require written consent and are processed via the usual enquiries route. :contentReference[oaicite:82]{index=82}

medium.question: Define ‘means of escape’ and ‘communal areas’.
medium.answer: Means of escape: routes leading safely out without external assistance; communal areas: spaces not directly controlled by an individual resident (corridors, laundries, lounges, car parks, etc.). :contentReference[oaicite:83]{index=83}

medium.question: What should Responsible Persons ensure at buildings/sites?
medium.answer: Suitable fire precautions, regular testing/records, staff training, housekeeping, and tracked mitigation action plans at regular review intervals. :contentReference[oaicite:84]{index=84}

hard.question: Draft a compliance plan for an HRRB with overdue communal fire-door checks and incomplete wayfinding signage.
hard.answer: Immediately schedule/complete communal door checks and annual front-door checks (11m+), update FRA actions and mitigation plans, install/verify wayfinding signage, ensure SIB and plans are in place, and obtain/display BAC; record testing/training/housekeeping and monitor actions at defined intervals. :contentReference[oaicite:85]{index=85}

hard.question: A resident repeatedly refuses access for a front-door check. How do you proceed within policy?
hard.answer: Apply ‘best endeavours’ (three attempts with written communications) and document each; continue engagement on reasonable adjustments to secure access. :contentReference[oaicite:86]{index=86}

hard.question: You inherit a mixed site (general needs, leasehold, agency-managed). What’s the overarching duty for FRAs?
hard.answer: Ensure suitable/sufficient FRAs for all buildings required by the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005 (as amended), covering relevant parties and areas where we retain responsibility. :contentReference[oaicite:87]{index=87}

hard.question: What elements should be included in HRRB documentation for first responders?
hard.answer: Secure Information Box, floor plans/building plans, maintained firefighters lifts/equipment, and wayfinding signage—kept current and accessible. :contentReference[oaicite:88]{index=88}

hard.question: How should external wall materials be treated in fire-risk work?
hard.answer: They must be considered and commented upon within the building’s Fire Risk Assessment. :contentReference[oaicite:89]{index=89}

hard.question: Outline the communication approach for building-wide FRA actions affecting access.
hard.answer: Notify residents via appropriate channels, offer reasonable adjustments for access, record attendance/testing, and provide fire safety instructions to residents in buildings with common areas. :contentReference[oaicite:90]{index=90}

hard.question: What core legislation underpins the policy?
hard.answer: Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005; Fire Safety Act 2021; Fire Safety (England) Regulations 2022; Building Safety Act 2022; Building Regulations Approved Document B; relevant British Standards. :contentReference[oaicite:91]{index=91}
